scope_note: Phase A only (S1-S6). Phase B (S7-S9) and v2 out of scope, blocked on ink v0 tag.

prior_learnings.title: Theme-registry id/folder mismatch fails via a broad assertion cascade, not a single targeted error

findings: TreatmentsSchema already requires banner: z.string() - S1 only adds a sibling optional `dynamic` key, not a change to banner itself.
Every nested zod object uses .strict() with no exceptions - new StyleToken/DynamicElement schemas must follow this at every nesting level.

Enum names (ElevationNameSchema etc.) are independently declared inline in schema.ts, NOT imported from treatments/* - 'kept in sync by hand' per the file's own header comment. S1's generator enum must follow this same convention, pinned by a themes-validation.test.ts describe block mirroring lines 732-765.

RawOverridesSchema uses z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()) as a deliberate escape hatch for untyped knobs - the spec's DynamicElement.render? perf-knobs likely needs the same escape hatch unless the field set is nailed down in story-planning.

treatments/* files are always pure lookup tables with only type-only Chakra imports - dynamicVisuals.ts breaks this precedent as the first file with a real runtime import (the ink package/stub) and the first with actual computation instead of static lookup. Flag explicitly in architecture.md as an intentional, sole exception.

iconSets.ts's resolveIconId() shows the established fail-soft-at-runtime pattern; validateManifest() shows fail-loud-at-build-time. Split for this epic: S1 schema/build gate fails loud on unknown generator name; S3's hook fails soft per AC-UX-3a (runtime fallback lives in the hook, not the adapter).

generate-theme-registry.mjs does ZERO value-level validation (only file-existence + folder-name regex, never imports/evaluates TS). The generator-catalog check (AC-VAL-1) belongs in themes-validation.test.ts's vitest suite, not in the .mjs codegen script - resolves an ambiguity in spec.md section 4.
Test file organizes describe blocks 1:1 with AC IDs - new S1/S6 tests should add new describe('AC-...') blocks, not fold into existing ones.
Zero existing Worker usage anywhere in the app (confirmed via grep) - S5 is a first-of-its-kind addition with no established convention or test-mocking infrastructure to extend.

next.config.mjs's only webpack customization is a babel-loader carve-out for 4 packages that crash SWC's optimizer - the only precedent for 'special-case webpack for a dependency', relevant if S5's worker needs its own loader rule.

key_files: specs/epic-pluggable-themes/architecture.md (prior epic's authoritative architecture doc - paradigm to reuse verbatim)
app/src/themes/schema.ts:1-19 (zod boundary: schema.ts is the ONLY module with a runtime zod import)
app/src/themes/contrast.ts (wcagRatio, evaluateThemeContrast, WCAG_AA_THRESHOLD=4.5 - reusable for AC-A11Y-1/2)
app/tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts:443-731 (resolved-import boundary scanner - reusable pattern for AC-STRUCT-4)
app/next.config.mjs (output:export confirmed, no worker-loader config exists)
findings: Paradigm (reuse verbatim from prior epic's architecture.md): package-by-feature per theme folder; shared infrastructure is layered n-tier + functional-core/imperative-shell (manifests/transforms pure = core; generator script + vitest gate = imperative shell). New modules slot into the imperative-shell layer, no new paradigm needed.

The zod/client-bundle boundary (AC-BOUND-1) is enforced by an automated resolved-import scanner in themes-validation.test.ts (not ESLint - no eslint config exists anywhere under app/), which parses imports, classifies runtime vs type-only, and resolves specifiers against schema.ts's absolute path. This is the exact reusable pattern for AC-STRUCT-4's single-import-site invariant on dynamicVisuals.ts - add a sibling test using the same resolver, target = the ink-package/stub specifier.

End-to-end static banner data flow traced: manifest.treatments.banner (literal SVG) -> generate-theme-registry.mjs (structural glob only) -> registry.generated.ts (static imports, committed) -> useThemeStyles.ts computeThemeStyles()/navBannerDecor() (pure, URL-encodes into data-URI, returns {boxProps, style} SPLIT because Chakra drops data-URI backgroundImage through its style pipeline) -> Layout.tsx (spreads at zIndex 0, behind nav Container at zIndex 1, logo Text color=brand.500 is leftmost child).

computeThemeStyles() must stay untouched/pure; useDynamicBanner.ts is a SEPARATE, second, client-only hook that Layout.tsx calls alongside useThemeStyles(), producing a second style object to conditionally overlay - never a modification of useThemeStyles.ts.

No existing Worker/worker-loader convention in next.config.mjs - Next.js 14.2.35's built-in `new Worker(new URL('../workers/banner.worker.ts', import.meta.url))` native ESM pattern is genuinely greenfield here, no plugin needed, static export does not preclude it.

contrast.ts's wcagRatio(hexA, hexB) is generic pure math (zero imports) and directly reusable for the scrim-vs-brand.500 contrast check; evaluateThemeContrast() itself is NOT directly reusable since it takes a ThemeManifest and checks manifest-declared color pairs, not arbitrary colors.

key_files: app/tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts (AC-ID-keyed describe blocks, string/regex SVG assertions)
findings: CRITICAL: this project has NO jsdom and NO React Testing Library installed at all (confirmed absent from package.json). No hook can be rendered/mounted in tests. Every existing hook is built as a thin useEffect/useState wrapper around an exported PLAIN FUNCTION that holds all actual decision logic - that plain function is what gets unit-tested, never the hook itself via a render. useDynamicBanner.ts MUST follow this same split (mirrors computeThemeStyles() being pure and separately tested from useThemeStyles()).

No Web Worker mocking precedent anywhere in the codebase - S5's worker tests are first-of-kind. Either mock the Worker global directly (novel infrastructure) or scope S6's automated tests to the requestIdleCallback/fallback path only, per the spec's own 'mechanism, not real timing' framing (already reflected in the AC-PERF-1/2 amendment).

No SVG parser exists in test suite; existing convention is lightweight string/regex matching on the encoded banner data (prefix checks, substring checks for viewBox presence / <script> absence). AC-STRUCT-6/AC-UX-5 assertions should follow this same string-matching style, not introduce a new SVG parsing dependency.
contrast.ts + WCAG_AA_THRESHOLD=4.5 is a real, reusable utility for AC-A11Y-1/2 - reuse wcagRatio() directly rather than re-deriving contrast math.
No i18n gap: feature is purely decorative, no new user-facing text; codebase convention is that purely-decorative elements carry no translated strings (only interactive/informative ones do).

E2E scope: the existing static banner already has visual-only (no-click) e2e coverage, so that category is precedented in this suite if ever wanted - but the epic's own phasing (S6 = unit tests against the stub only, deeper visual/perf verification deferred) is a legitimate spec-driven scoping choice already confirmed by spec validation, not a testing-convention gap.
